home.tip2Description: Packing less reduces fuel use and lowers the environmental...
home.tip3Description: Small choices before and after your trip can make your...

tip1Article.intro: Flying with GreenWing already means choosing a greener path — but understanding how CO₂ emissions work can help you make even smarter travel decisions.
tip1Article.para1: Aviation accounts for around 2.5% of global CO₂ emissions, but its overall climate impact is estimated to be significantly higher when you factor in contrails, nitrogen oxides, and other high-altitude effects. By choosing more efficient routes, newer aircraft, and sustainable fuel options, GreenWing actively works to minimize this impact on every flight.
tip1Article.para2: Every flight you take with us contributes to our ongoing investment in Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) and carbon offset programmes. SAF can reduce lifecycle CO₂ emissions by up to 80% compared to conventional jet fuel. We publish transparent emissions data for every route so you always know the environmental cost of your journey.

keyTakeaways.title: What you can do
keyTakeaways.point1: Choose direct flights — takeoff and landing produce the most emissions
keyTakeaways.point2: Travel in economy class — fewer seats mean a higher carbon share per passenger in business
keyTakeaways.point3: Offset your flight through our in-app carbon calculator
keyTakeaways.point4: Check our route emissions before booking at greenwing.com/sustainability

tip2Article.intro: The weight of your luggage has a direct impact on how much fuel an aircraft burns — and therefore how much CO₂ is released into the atmosphere.
tip2Article.para1: Every extra kilogram on a flight increases fuel consumption. Studies suggest that reducing average baggage weight across a full aircraft by just 1 kg can save hundreds of kilograms of CO₂ per year. When millions of passengers make small changes, the cumulative effect is enormous. Packing smart isn't just convenient — it's one of the easiest ways to reduce your travel footprint.
tip2Article.para2: Traveling light also means less time at baggage claim, no risk of lost luggage, and lower costs. Our carry-on allowance covers everything most travellers need for a short European break. Challenge yourself to pack only what you'll actually use — you might be surprised how little that is.

keyTakeaways.title: Tips for packing lighter
keyTakeaways.point1: Stick to carry-on only for trips under 5 days
keyTakeaways.point2: Wear your heaviest items on the plane
keyTakeaways.point3: Choose versatile, lightweight clothing that can be mixed and matched
keyTakeaways.point4: Use solid toiletries to save weight and avoid liquid restrictions

tip3Article.intro: Sustainable travel doesn't begin and end at the airport. The choices you make before you leave home and after you arrive can significantly reduce the overall environmental impact of your trip.
tip3Article.para1: Getting to and from the airport is often overlooked in travel carbon calculations. Taking a train or bus instead of a private car to the airport can cut your transfer emissions by up to 90%. At your destination, choosing public transport, cycling, or walking over taxis and rental cars makes a meaningful difference — and often gives you a more authentic experience of the places you visit.
tip3Article.para2: Your accommodation choices matter too. Look for hotels with green certifications, or consider staying in locally-owned guesthouses that reinvest in the community. Simple habits like reusing towels, turning off air conditioning when you leave, and avoiding single-use plastics all add up. Every conscious decision you make as a traveller sends a signal to the industry that sustainability is a priority.

keyTakeaways.title: Easy sustainable habits
keyTakeaways.point1: Take public transport or train to the airport
keyTakeaways.point3: Use refillable water bottles and avoid single-use plastic
keyTakeaways.point4: Support local businesses and restaurants at your destination

greenWingPass.title: Join GreenWing Pass
greenWingPass.subtitle: The only airline that lets you travel across Europe with zero emissions and with one membership.
greenWingPass.description: GreenWing Pass is a monthly subscription that lets you fly up to 3 times per year within Europe, on 100% emission-free flights.
greenWingPass.feature1Desc: Use them whenever you want, across our European network.
greenWingPass.feature2Desc: Every flight is designed to minimize its environmental impact.
greenWingPass.feature3Desc: You pay the same amount every month, with no surprises.
greenWingPass.feature4Desc: Book flights, manage your trips, and track your positive impact.

help.heroSubtitle: Search for answers or browse our help center
help.searchPlaceholder: Search for help articles, FAQs, or topics...
help.noResultsDescription: Try different keywords or browse our articles below

article1.title: What happens after I complete a payment?
article1.description: Step-by-step explanation of what happens once your payment is successful.

content.intro: After you complete a payment, our system automatically verifies the transaction and confirms your booking.
content.para1: You'll be redirected back to our platform once the payment is processed. This usually takes just a few seconds.
content.para2: As soon as we receive confirmation from the payment provider, your booking status will update and a confirmation email will be sent to you.

article2.title: Payment completed but booking not confirmed
article2.description: What to do if your payment went through but you don't see a confirmation.

content.intro: Sometimes payment confirmation can take a little longer than expected. Don't worry — your money is safe.
content.para1: In rare cases, there may be a delay between the payment provider and our system.
content.para2: If your booking is not confirmed within 15 minutes, we recommend checking your email (including spam folder).

keyTakeaways.title: Recommended actions
keyTakeaways.point2: Check your email inbox and spam
keyTakeaways.point4: Contact support if the issue persists

article3.title: Why was my payment declined?
article3.description: Common reasons why payments fail and how to fix them.

content.intro: Payment failures are usually related to your bank or card provider, not our platform.
content.para1: Your bank may block a transaction for security reasons, especially for international payments.
content.para2: Insufficient funds, incorrect card details, or expired cards are also common causes.

article4.title: How membership works on our platform
article4.description: Everything you need to know about membership and processing times.

content.intro: membership eligibility depends on the airline, rental provider, or property you booked with.
content.para1: If your booking is refundable, we'll process the membership as soon as the provider confirms it.
content.para2: membership usually take 5–10 business days to appear on your account, depending on your bank.

keyTakeaways.title: membership basics
keyTakeaways.point3: Funds return to original payment method

article5.title: How membership payments work
article5.description: Clear explanation of our membership model and billing cycle.

content.intro: Our membership is designed for frequent travelers who want predictable pricing and flexibility.
content.para1: With an active membership, you get access to three free flights covered by your monthly fee.
content.para2: The membership renews automatically each month and cannot be canceled before one year.

article6.title: Can I cancel my Membership?
article6.description: Important information about membership cancellation rules.

content.intro: We believe in transparency, so it's important to understand membership commitments upfront.
content.para1: membership cannot be canceled during the first year of activation.
content.para2: After the one-year period, you can cancel anytime from your account settings.

article7.title: What if my flight is canceled by the airline?
article7.description: Your options when airlines cancel or change flights.

content.intro: Airline cancellations happen, and we're here to help you navigate the next steps.
content.para1: In most cases, airlines offer a refund or rebooking option.
content.para2: We'll notify you as soon as we receive an update from the airline.

article8.title: How to change or correct passenger details
article8.description: Learn how name changes and corrections work.

content.intro: Passenger details must match official travel documents exactly.
content.para1: Minor corrections may be allowed depending on airline rules.
content.para2: Some airlines charge a fee for name changes or do not allow them at all.

article9.title: Is my payment information secure?
article9.description: How we protect your data and transactions.

content.intro: Security is a top priority for us.
content.para1: All payments are processed through certified payment providers.
content.para2: We never store your full card details on our servers.

article10.title: Booking flights vs apartments and cars

content.intro: Each booking type follows slightly different rules and policies.
content.para1: Flight bookings are governed by airline policies.
content.para2: Apartments and car rentals follow partner-specific terms.

article11.title: Didn't receive your confirmation email?
article11.description: What to do if your confirmation email is missing.

content.intro: Confirmation emails are sent automatically after booking.
content.para1: Email delivery can sometimes be delayed by providers.
content.para2: Always check your spam or promotions folder.

article12.title: How to contact support
article12.description: When and how to reach our support team.

content.intro: Our support team is here to help when you need it.
content.para1: We recommend checking the Help Center first for instant answers.
content.para2: If you still need assistance, contact us through your account.

category1.title: Booking & Payment
category1.q1: What payment methods do you accept?
category1.a1: We accept all major credit cards...
category1.a2: Yes, you can change your booking...

category2.title: GreenWing Pass
category2.a1: GreenWing Pass is a monthly subscription...
category2.a3: Yes, you can cancel your subscription...

category3.title: Flights & Travel
category3.a1: Economy class: 1 cabin bag (10kg) and 1 checked bag (23kg). Business class: 2 cabin bags (20kg) and 2 checked bags (32kg each).
category3.q2: How early should I arrive at the airport?
category3.a2: We recommend arriving 2 hours before departure for domestic flights and 3 hours for international flights.

category4.title: Sustainability
category4.q1: How are your flights zero emission?
category4.a1: We use sustainable aviation fuel (SAF), invest in carbon offset programs, and continuously upgrade to more efficient aircraft.
category4.a2: Our environmental policy focuses on achieving net-zero emissions by 2030 through innovation, sustainable fuels, and carbon offsetting.
